The steam clings to my hair, but I let it bead and drip down the glass
Droplets run through flour-dust on the glass, blurring 7th Avenue while my unpeeled daikon sits in the crate
Droplets run through flour-dust on the glass, distorting 7th Avenue while my chest feels hollow and spacious
The white space on the calendar square feels larger than the Pacific
The morning sun hitting the tip of the Transamerica Pyramid while my street is still swaddled in grey
